description:
## Goal

Read the inflow histograms diagnostically, verify fz-t45 dissolves,
final polish.

## What lands

### --Wspec-quality reads histograms
- src/ir_typer.rs / wherever --Wspec-quality is wired (fz-aiz.6):
  for each canonical, for each slot, inspect `inflows`. A slot whose
  LUB is Tagged but whose inflow histogram is dominated by a narrower
  repr (e.g., RawInt: 99, Tagged: 1) is reported as a split candidate:
  "consider splitting fn `<name>`: 99% RawInt callers paying Tagged
  tax for 1 polymorphic caller."
- Threshold for "dominant": configurable; default sensible (e.g., 90%).

### Close fz-t45
- Run the fz-t45 repro: `each` + non-empty closure captures, expects
  `11 / 12 / 13`. Verify correct output under JIT and AOT.
- Confirm by inspection: closure values' body_fn_id wires to
  canonical's FuncId; indirect-dispatch sig is all-Tagged i64;
  canonical ABI for opaque-reachable fns is Tagged by construction.
  Wire matches semantics. Mismatch structurally impossible.
- `bw close fz-t45` with reference to this ticket.

### Sweep
- Rip the now-unused `input_descrs` param from `SpecRegistry::resolve`
  if all callers tolerate the signature change. Update callsites.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`.
- `cargo fmt`.
- Full `cargo test` pass.

## Acceptance

- `--Wspec-quality` emits actionable per-slot reports across all
  fixtures; at least one fixture surfaces a meaningful candidate.
- fz-t45 closed with verified repro.
- No clippy warnings; fmt clean.
- All 618 unit + 166 fixture-matrix tests pass.

## See also

- fz-7ot.2 — populates the canonical/histogram data this consumes.
- fz-aiz.6 — original --Wspec-quality wiring.
- fz-t45 — closure ABI mismatch; verified dissolved here.

comments.text: Arc halted at fz-b9b. Diagnostic + close fz-t45 + sweep deferred until the canonical-first redesign is re-approached (likely as a codegen-side collapse rather than typer-side, per fz-b9b post-mortem). fz-t45 remains open.

title: fz-7ot.3: diagnostic + close fz-t45 + sweep
